qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Caller-supplied cart numbers let anonymous users read and modify credentialed carts
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a public cart authorization bypass in CartController::_getCart()/actionLoadCart(), while the advisory describes authenticated SQL injection in InventoryController::actionInventoryLevelsTableData() where sort parameters reach addOrderBy() without validation.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No finalized qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable inventory sorting code path or its SQL-concatenation root cause.
evidence: GHSA-pmgj-gmm4-jh6j describes SQL injection in src/controllers/InventoryController.php:252-266, where attacker-controlled sort[0][sortField] and sort[0][direction] are concatenated into addOrderBy().
The referenced fix commit 094d69df24b925544f337c38e2ec1effcd5395c7 adds an allowlist for the inventory sort field and direction in actionInventoryLevelsTableData(), confirming that root cause.
.judge/input_manifest.json lists only one finalized qualifying finding: active_cart_number_takeover.

active_cart_number_takeover instead targets src/controllers/CartController.php:371 and :604 plus src/services/Carts.php:242, describing anonymous cart takeover by caller-supplied order number rather than any inventory sorting or SQL-construction path.
